degraded: classify_complexity reported risk flag 'touches_auth' — FALSE POSITIVE. The classifier matches risk keywords against the message prose, and the substring 'auth' occurs inside 'FR-authoring'. No auth file is in the diff; the risk_detectors.py diff-predicates are authoritative and F11 recomputes them. Overridden deliberately.

External code review: first invocation returned a truncated Gemini response and an OpenAI 'NoneType' error. Retried; the retry succeeded with both providers (2/2) and its findings were acted on. Recorded because the first attempt was reported as reviews_succeeded=1 with degraded=false, which understates it.

Local-only test-environment failures, NOT regressions and NOT caused by this diff: shipwright-security (3) needs semgrep/gitleaks/trivy on PATH and shipwright-grade (4) needs recorded empirical fixtures. Both suites are fully green without CI=true (470 passed/3 skipped and 393 passed/5 skipped); the CI-discipline rule promotes those environment skips to hard failures when CI=true is set locally. Neither plugin contains a file touched by this diff.

CodeQL flagged a REAL high-severity py/redos in this diff's own new code (group_i_detectors._FILE_PATH_RE: `[\w./-]*\w\.(ext)` overlaps its own class). Root-fixed by anchoring on the extension (linear, no leading quantifier) and by removing the same overlap class from _PASCAL_RE — NOT suppressed. Regression-tested and re-probed for accuracy. A second, MEDIUM semgrep alert (non-literal-import in shared/scripts/tools/verifiers/_layer_coverage_regen.py) is PRE-EXISTING and outside this diff.

surface_verification.justification: Framework-internal change: an agent-authoring rulebook plus prompt/reference edits in adopt/project/iterate, and a new advisory compliance audit group. No product source, API route, store, SSE/WS handler, message contract or UI code is touched, so the backend-affects-frontend rule does not fire and there is no runtime surface to drive. Verified instead by the empirical probes recorded in the iterate spec, including running the detectors against the real webui spec before and after its human cleanup (66 rows/92% dirty -> 29 rows/0% dirty, zero false positives) and a mutation probe proving the drift guard fires.

behaviors.behavior: name fence flags HTTP verb / ADR / iterate slug / file path / snake_case symbol
clean capability name produces no finding
description fence flags file path and camelCase symbol
plain prose with a parenthetical gloss passes clean
platform words (iOS/iPadOS) are not treated as code symbols
PascalCase symbols flagged; Title Case prose is not
every fold verb the rulebook names is detected
'Phase N of' in domain prose is not a fold candidate
header-driven scan reads greenfield / adopt / Area table shapes
Removed Requirements rows are excluded from prose linting
Source column (legitimate file paths) is never linted
I1 skips (not passes) when the spec shape has no Name column
I1 genuinely passes on a clean spec that has a Name column
duplicate FR ID within a split is reported
reuse of a retired FR number is caught (fr-authoring.md section 4)
absent spec SKIPs rather than fails
advisory contract: group I, detective-only, never HIGH
prose checks never emit fail, so they never flip the audit verdict
preview capped at 5 IDs while the true count is reported
Group I is registered and runs in the default group set
behaviors.evidence: test_audit_detector.py + test_audit_group_b.py + test_audit_groups_a_d.py (pins updated to A-I) PASSED

behaviors.behavior: Group I is inside the Stop-hook full-coverage gate (no false dismiss)
rulebook exists, is non-empty, and retains every cited section
all four FR-authoring surfaces still cite the rulebook
adopt's contradicting guidance stays removed AND the new rule is positively present
both iterate paths still carry the MINT-vs-FOLD gate
agents actually author better FR prose given the new instructions
behaviors.evidence: LLM prose quality has no deterministic oracle. Mitigated structurally: Group I measures the OUTPUT, and the real-spec probe shows it discriminates human-clean from human-dirty specs with zero false positives.

behaviors.behavior: the audit group registry, the detector's default run set, and the Stop-hook coverage gate agree on the group set — so a newly registered group cannot run while the gate still reports full coverage and dismisses the backlog item
behaviors.evidence: shared/tests/test_audit_group_registry_stop_gate_integration.py (3 tests, real registry + real run_all + real coverage_ok) PASSED. Mutation-probed: reverting _EXPECTED_GROUPS to {A..H} fails it, restoring passes — it catches the exact HIGH false-dismiss the review round found.

behaviors.behavior: the prose detectors run in linear time on pathological input (no ReDoS) — they scan arbitrary spec text, and adopt reverse-engineers that text from a foreign repo, so a hung detector would wedge the whole compliance audit
behaviors.evidence: test_audit_group_i_detectors.py::test_detectors_are_linear_on_pathological_input PASSED (5 hostile inputs incl. 4000-char word runs, < 2s ceiling)

behaviors.behavior: the ReDoS fix did not weaken file-path detection, incl. .tsx not read as .ts + x
behaviors.evidence: test_file_path_detection_survived_the_redos_fix + test_tsx_is_not_read_as_ts_plus_x PASSED; real-spec probe re-run unchanged at 45%/93% dirty vs 0%/0% clean
